llm_judge_candidates.truth_value: κ-carbide precipitates first from austenitic matrix. Cu atoms are rejected from κ-carbide, enriching the surrounding matrix. When Cu concentration reaches critical level, Cu clusters form and precipitate as Cu-rich phase. Cu-rich phase nucleates in austenitic matrix near κ-carbide due to lower misfit with matrix (1.0%) compared to with κ-carbide (3.4%). The presence of κ-carbide promotes Cu-rich phase precipitation. Addition of Cu increases austenite lattice parameter, promoting κ-carbide precipitation.
llm_judge_candidates.prediction_value: κ-carbide precipitates first from austenitic matrix; Cu atoms migrate to austenitic matrix without κ-carbide, increasing Cu concentration near κ-carbide; as Cu concentration reaches a certain level, Cu atoms gather and form Cu-rich phase precipitates.
